key_insight: A verified knowledge-based algorithm for pancreatic cancer staging outperformed a retrieval-augmented LLM on accuracy while also being faster in a simulated comparison.
clinical_relevance: For guideline-driven staging tasks, deterministic rule-based decision support may be more reliable and efficient than LLM assistance in clinical workflows.
method: Implemented a web-based knowledge-based algorithm covering TNM, stage, and resectability classification using current Japanese guidelines.
Exhaustively checked the system’s outputs across all possible inputs.
Compared unassisted, LLM-assisted, and KBA-assisted staging by six non-board-certified radiologists across 12 simulated cases.
evidence_signal: In the comparative experiment, KBA assistance achieved 98.6% staging accuracy and 196.2 s mean staging time, versus 81.9%/229.2 s unassisted and 80.6%/401.9 s with LLM assistance.
watch_item: Results come from a small simulated-case study, so real-world performance, generalizability, and workflow integration still need validation.
paper_title: Algorithmic implementation of pancreatic cancer staging guidelines: comparison with a retrieval-augmented large language model
